title: Handlers in a single try-catch or function-try-block for a derived class and some or all of its bases should be ordered most-derived-first
description: Handlers in a single try-catch or function-try-block for a derived class and some or all of its bases should be ordered most-derived-firstAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEcertmisra-c++2008Why is this an issue?More InfoExceptions handlers (catch ()) are evaluated in the order they are written. Once a match is found, the evaluation stops. If there is a handler for a base class followed by a handler for class derived from that base class, the second handler will never trigger: The handler for the base class will match the derived class, and will be the only executed handler.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Handlers in a single try-catch should be ordered most-derived-first
